message: Node started: Find the smallest prime p such that there exists an integer n where n^4 + 1 is divisible by p^2. To do this, test primes p in strictly increasing order starting from p=2. For each p, check if there exists an n in the range 1 to (p^2 - 1) such that n^4 + 1 is congruent to 0 modulo p^2. Stop immediately upon finding the first valid p. Conclude by performing the arithmetic check: calculate (n^4 + 1) mod p^2 to verify it equals 0 for your chosen n and p.
